name: Kansas Respiratory Therapist License
description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Kansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Kansas Board of Healing Arts — RT
provider.parentOrganization: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS)

address.description:
Kansas State Board of Healing Arts
800 SW Jackson, Lower Level - Suite A
Topeka, KS 66612

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Kansas respiratory therapist license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.
Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Kansas have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Kansas. To work in Kansas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Kansas?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Kansas while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
